title: Equus Star Navy
content:
The Equus Star Navy was one of the the main service branches of Equus Military Forces, designated as overseeing all space-capable military vessels and space-to-space marine operations. Following the Great War, it emerged as one of the main space forces of the Hydian Sector.

 

The ESN was converted into Imperial Navy in 1346 CYP (2671 CE)

History

Background

The need for a unified Equus was quickly realized following the 1st Battle of Yildun Rest and the start of the Great War, leading to the Convocation of Creatures voting unanimously to form a world government on May 15th, 2429 CE. This decision did not immediately include any armed forces, leaving command of all space vessels to the pre-established, civilian Equestrian Space Agency (ESA). A month later, on June 14th, the Equus High Command and the Equus Military Force (EMF) are formalized, transferring all military assets into two main branches: the Equus Planetary Forces (EPF) and the Equus Star Navy (ESN). The ESN received all of the ESA's fleet, barring only the ESAV Starswirl. Prism Gleam and Nebula Bolt were designated as the ESN's the first ranking admirals, with Prism Gleam appointed as the commander-in-chief of the EMF.

The Great War

Within the first 6 months of the Great War, the majority of the fleet (consisting of mostly Manehattan-Class  and Cloudsdale I-class ships) had been destroyed or rendered unusable. The ESN, along with most of the rest of the Sector Alliance, was forced to withdraw most of it's forces away from the front line for much needed upgrades and repair, including the installation of advanced Preserver shield technology. During this period, the first mainline combat vessel would be launched: the ESS Cloudsdale-A, the first Cloudsdale II-class Cruiser. The Cloudsdale II-Class would quickly become the backbone of the Equus Navy for the majority of the Great War. The next few years would see the introduction of more major ship classes: the Fillydelphia-Class Escort in 2430, the Canterlot-Class Command Cruiser in 2431, and the Baltimare-Class Escort and Vanhoover-Class Carrier in 2433.
